product_overview: Draftr converts URLs or PDFs into short-form brainrot-style vertical videos via an AI pipeline and provides a browsable library of generated shorts for guest and authenticated users.
core_goals: Convert user-provided content (URLs or PDFs) into a batch of short vertical videos.
Allow users to browse and consume generated shorts in a TikTok-style vertical feed organized by chat session.
Provide a friction-free guest experience so users can try the product without signing in.
Surface user account information, generation history, and library access for authenticated users.
Enable simple navigation between product sections (Home, Chat, Shorts, About, Profile).

features.name: Guest Mode
features.description: Let visitors use the app without signing in, generating into and browsing a shared general library.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> See the login card offering a guest option -> Choose to continue as guest -> Navigate to /shorts -> See the General library label and browse shared shorts
Navigate directly to /shorts as an unauthenticated user -> See the sidebar labeled 'General library' and browse available shorts

features.name: Sign Out
features.description: Allow authenticated users to end their session and revert to guest view.
user_flows: Navigate to /profile while authenticated -> See account info and sign-out option -> Choose to log out -> Observe session is cleared and UI returns to guest state
After logging out, navigate to /profile -> See upsell message for guests and a Login option

features.name: AI Chat Video Generation
features.description: Users submit content (URL or PDF) and start a generation batch that streams progress in the conversation and produces multiple short videos.
user_flows: Navigate to /chat -> See the hero heading describing content-to-video generation -> Paste a URL into the prompt input -> Set the desired video count using the stepper -> Submit the prompt to start generation -> Watch live batch status stream appear in the conversation -> Navigate to /shorts -> See the newly generated shorts associated with the chat

Navigate to /chat -> See the hero heading describing content-to-video generation -> Upload a PDF file as the source content -> Set the desired video count using the stepper -> Submit the PDF to start generation -> Watch live batch status stream appear in the conversation -> Navigate to /shorts -> See the newly generated shorts associated with the chat

features.name: Shorts Library Viewer
features.description: A vertical, TikTok-style feed where users browse generated shorts, inspect metadata, and engage with videos.
user_flows: Navigate to /shorts -> See the sidebar with a list of chat sessions -> Select a chat session -> See that chat's shorts load into the main vertical feed (one 9:16 card visible at a time)

While viewing a short in the feed -> Scroll or swipe to navigate to the next short -> Tap the video to toggle play/pause -> Tap the mute control to toggle audio -> Toggle the like control to change liked state
Navigate to /shorts -> Select a playing short -> Open the metadata panel to view title, source, duration and style -> Choose to open the video in a new tab to view it standalone

features.name: User Profile
features.description: Profile view surfaces identity, account stats, recent chats and links into the user's library; guests see an upsell and login prompt.
user_flows: Navigate to /profile while authenticated -> See display name and avatar -> See stats cards for saved shorts, generation runs, active chats and failed runs -> See a recent chats list with titles and sources -> Choose Open your library -> Navigate to /shorts and view personal library
Navigate to /profile while unauthenticated -> See an upsell message and a prompt to log in -> Choose to open login -> Navigate to /login

features.name: Navbar Navigation
features.description: Persistent top navigation to access major sections and view auth state.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Use the navbar to go to /chat -> Observe the chat page
From any page, use the navbar to go to /shorts -> Observe the shorts library
From any page, use the navbar to go to /about -> Observe the about page
From any page, use the navbar to open profile -> Navigate to /profile and observe avatar or login state

features.name: Home Page — Marketing
features.description: Landing page that explains the product and links users into the generation flow.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See the hero headline and product description -> Use the 'Generate your first video' CTA to go to /chat -> Observe the chat generation page
Navigate to / -> See feature cards and demo mockup -> Use the 'See how it works' CTA to go to /about -> Observe the about page

tech_stack.ui_library: CSS Modules, Lucide React, Framer Motion

routes.description: Marketing home page with hero, features, and how-it-works sections
Login page with Google OAuth sign-in and guest continue options
Supabase OAuth callback route that exchanges code for session
AI chat page for generating brainrot short videos from URLs or PDFs
Shorts library viewer with TikTok-style feed, chat list sidebar, and recommendation insights
User profile page showing account info, stats, and recent chats; guest users see upsell
About page with video hero explaining the product

features.name: Google OAuth Login
features.description: Sign in with Google via Supabase OAuth. Redirects through /auth/callback to exchange code for session.
user_interactions: See the login card with "Sign in when you want your own library" headline
Click "Login with Google" button to initiate Google OAuth
See error message if Google sign-in fails
Click "Continue as guest" to skip login and proceed as guest

features.name: Guest Mode
features.description: Users can skip login and use the app as a guest, accessing the shared general library.
user_interactions: Click "Continue as guest" on the login page
Use the app without signing in
See "General library" label in the UI

features.name: Sign Out
features.description: Authenticated users can sign out from the profile page.
user_interactions: Click "Log out" button on the profile page
Session is cleared and user is returned to guest mode

features.name: AI Chat Video Generation
features.description: Users paste a URL or upload a PDF and the backend generates brainrot short videos via CrewAI + OpenAI pipeline.
user_interactions: See the hero heading "Turn your content into brainrot."
Type or paste a URL into the prompt input box
Upload a PDF file
Adjust the video count (5–15) using the stepper
Click the send/submit button to start generation
Watch live batch status stream in the conversation
See error message if generation fails

features.name: Shorts Library Viewer
features.description: TikTok-style vertical video feed showing generated shorts. Users can browse chats in a sidebar and watch their shorts.
user_interactions: See the sidebar with list of chat sessions
Click a chat to load its shorts
Scroll/swipe to navigate between shorts in the feed
Click the video to play/pause
Click the mute button to toggle audio
Click the like button to like a short
Click the info button to see metadata panel
Click the open button to open the video in a new tab

features.name: User Profile
features.description: Profile page showing user identity, account stats, recent chats, and sign-out button for authenticated users. Shows upsell for guests.
user_interactions: See display name and avatar
See stats (saved shorts, generation runs, active chats, failed runs)
See recent chat activity list
Click "Open your library" to go to shorts
Click "Log out" to sign out
Guest sees upsell message and "Login" button

features.name: Navbar Navigation
features.description: Persistent top navigation bar with links to all major sections and auth state display.
user_interactions: Click "Draftr" logo to go to home
Click "Chat" link to go to chat
Click "Shorts" link to go to shorts library
Click "About" link to go to about page
Click user avatar/name to go to profile
See login button if not authenticated

known_limitations.issue: Google OAuth requires NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true; if not set, sign-in redirects to an error page
known_limitations.impact: Login with Google will fail unless environment variable is configured correctly

known_limitations.issue: Video generation requires a running backend (FastAPI/brainrot) service; if backend is down, batch creation fails
known_limitations.impact: Chat generation will show an error if the backend is not reachable

known_limitations.issue: Engagement tracking is best-effort; failures are silently swallowed
known_limitations.impact: Watch-time and retention data may be incomplete
